Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log. The goal: produce a process tree that captures the process behavior. Given event logs: Case 1: RA, VF, AE, RMI, VF, AD, SC, AC. Case 2: RA, VF, AE, AD, SC, AC. Case 3: RA, VF, AE, RMI, RMI, VF, AD, SC, AC. Observations: All start with RA Then VF Then AE Then sometimes a loop: RMI and then possibly VF again, repeated zero or more times. In case 1: one RMI, then VF, then AD. In case 3: two RMI s, then VF, then AD. There is also the possibility to go directly from AE to AD as seen in case 2.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the main process: Sequence of RA -> VF -> AE -> Loop: (RMI -> VF) * -> AD -> SC -> AC. But also we have potential concurrency? No concurrency observed. So it is sequential.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the process tree: root is a sequence: RA -> VF -> AE -> loop -> AD -> SC -> AC. Inside loop: the loop operator with body A = RMI, and B = VF. Usually the loop operator works as: do body A, then either exit or do B then repeat. * ( A, B ) is a loop: So the process tree A is executed, then either you exit the loop, or you execute B and then A again. The pattern is: A (B A)* The possible sequences from the loop are: A (exit), A B A (exit), A B A B A (exit), etc.

Name: Validation - NE
Text: This yields sequences like: RMI, VF, RMI, VF, but not RMI, RMI, VF. To allow repeated RMI before VF, we need to incorporate another structure.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: One way: loop body could be (RMI, VF) with exit after VF; Alternatively, we can treat the loop as: * ( (RMI, VF) , tau ).

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Thus process tree: Sequence ( RA, VF, AE, XOR (tau, Loop ( Sequence(RMI, VF), tau ) ), AD, SC, AC ).

Name: Validation - NE
Text: In case 3 (two RMI and VF pairs) loop executes twice: RMI VF RMI VF. Thus this process tree correctly captures observed behavior.
